hero.question: Will Anthropic have the best AI model at the end of March 2026?
hero.prob_take: The 3.9 point drop signals real money flowing toward Claude's competitors, likely spooked by OpenAI's o1 reasoning chops and the buzz around GPT-5 roadmap whispers hitting the rumor mill. At 89.8%, you're paying for Anthropic to nail execution in 14 months while being fully hedged against any surprise from the two most well-funded labs on Earth, which feels optimistic given how volatile this race actually is.

daily_take.headline: Anthropic's AI Crown Slipping, But 90% Odds Still Smell Like Easy Money
daily_take.deck: Anthropic dropped 3.9 points overnight to 89.8%, likely spooked by fresh Claude benchmarks or competitive noise from OpenAI/Google. Here's the thing: the market is pricing in a coin flip outcome as near-certainty. "Best model" is vague enough that Anthropic wins if Claude stays competitive, not dominant. That 90% floor looks like it hasn't priced in real competition risk. Smart money nibbles shorts here or waits for 92%+ to go full hedge.
